ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class; no specific Boston Kreme or cream-filled chocolate-frosted doughnut subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039V covers doughnuts as a category; no specific filled/frosted variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for Boston Kreme donut or cream-filled doughnut at the required specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Boston Kreme doughnut weighing approximately 85 g, as sold by Dunkin' Donuts.

nutrient_source.title: Dunkin' Donuts Boston Kreme Donut — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Dunkin' Donuts Boston Kreme Donut (FDC ID 2103697 / similar branded entry). Values normalised to per-100g basis from the 85g serving data reported. Where specific micronutrients are not reported in the branded entry, sentinel value -1 is used.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake-type — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Dunkin' Boston Kreme donut specifically. The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for cake-type and yeast-raised doughnuts in the range of 75–76. A GI of 76 is applied here as the closest defensible analogous value for a yeast-raised, filled, and frosted doughnut. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 44.0) / 100 ≈ 33.5, using 44.0 g carbohydrate in an 85 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Each Boston Kreme donut provides approximately 270 mg sodium (85 g serving), contributing around 12% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and account for cumulative sodium from other dietary sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index, high sugar content, and refined flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 76 and approximately 20 g of sugar per serving, the Boston Kreme donut causes a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise and should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, portion control and pairing with protein or fibre-rich foods may partially attenuate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, added sugar, and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Boston Kreme donut is an ultra-processed food providing approximately 5 g saturated fat and 20 g added sugar per serving; regular consumption is associated with increased LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk, and individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise intake in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Boston Kreme donut is a classic American yeast-raised doughnut produced by Dunkin' Donuts, named after the Boston cream pie dessert it emulates. It consists of a soft, deep-fried enriched wheat flour dough ring that is injected with a smooth vanilla Bavarian cream (custard-style) filling and topped with a glossy chocolate fondant or ganache frosting. The texture is light and pillowy on the inside with a slightly chewy exterior, while the filling is rich and creamy and the chocolate topping adds a sweet, fudgy contrast. In Singapore, it is sold at Dunkin' Donuts outlets such as the Funan location and is priced at approximately SGD 2.90. Nutritionally, it is an energy-dense, ultra-processed snack food providing around 353 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (approximately 52 g/100 g), moderate fat (approximately 14 g/100 g), and significant added sugar (approximately 24 g/100 g). Its high glycemic index (approximately 76) makes it a high-glycaemic food that causes rapid blood glucose elevation. It is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease without strict portion control. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with relevant allergies or intolerances.

How many calories in a Dunkin Boston Kreme?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for Dunkin' Donuts Boston Kreme Donut, which is the closest exact branded match available in an approved source. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database for cake-type and yeast-raised doughnuts, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are normalised to per-100g from an 85g serving as reported in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for Dunkin' Donuts Boston Kreme Donut. Minor rounding applied. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA branded source entry. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 8. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 11. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 19. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 20. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 21. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 22. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded source entry for this product. 23. This is an American-origin commercial product sold in Singapore; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are null as the product is marketed exclusively under its English brand name 'Boston Kreme' with no established local-language equivalents. The G6 multilingual rule applies to Singaporean dishes; this is an American commercial product and the rule does not mandate local-language names for non-Singaporean cuisine items sold in Singapore. 24. Halal status is listed as false as Dunkin' Donuts Singapore halal certification status requires verification at the outlet level and cannot be confirmed from approved sources at the time of processing.
